# ============================================================
# Overleaf Upload List — Part 1 Report
# ============================================================
# Upload ALL files listed below to your Overleaf project.
# Main file to compile: report_part1.tex
# Compiler: pdfLaTeX (Overleaf default)
# ============================================================

# ── LaTeX source ────────────────────────────────────────────
report_part1.tex                          (MAIN FILE — compile this)

# ── Plot images (generated by plot_results.py) ─────────────
# Copy from:  part1/results/<filename>
# Upload to:  Overleaf root (same level as report_part1.tex)

results/fig1_accuracy_vs_K.pdf           → fig1_accuracy_vs_K.pdf
results/fig2_params_vs_K.pdf             → fig2_params_vs_K.pdf
results/fig3_fb_dcf_accuracy_vs_K.pdf    → fig3_fb_dcf_accuracy_vs_K.pdf
results/fig4_fb_atoms.pdf                → fig4_fb_atoms.pdf
results/fig5_dcf_atoms.pdf               → fig5_dcf_atoms.pdf
results/fig6_psnr_vs_K.pdf               → fig6_psnr_vs_K.pdf
results/fig7_explained_variance.pdf      → fig7_explained_variance.pdf

# ── Notes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. Overleaf supports PDF images directly with \includegraphics.
# 2. The \includegraphics commands in report_part1.tex already
#    use the filenames listed above (no path prefix needed,
#    assuming all files are in the Overleaf project root).
# 3. If you prefer PNG for faster preview, re-run plot_results.py
#    with the savefig extension changed from .pdf to .png, and
#    update the \includegraphics references in report_part1.tex.
# 4. No external .bib file is needed — the bibliography is
#    embedded in the .tex using the thebibliography environment.
# ============================================================
